cards.name: Launch Now, Explain Later
cards.text: Gain momentum by leaning into urgency.

cards.name: First-Principles Reset
cards.text: Strip the argument to its bare assumptions.

cards.name: Backup Civilization
cards.text: Argue from long-term survival at civilization scale.

cards.name: Engagement Multiplier
cards.text: Optimize for connection, scale, and participation.

cards.name: Network Effects
cards.text: A platform becomes more valuable because others use it.

cards.name: Terms Accepted
cards.text: Proceed without fully explaining the trade-offs.

cards.name: Citation Needed
cards.text: Demand support for a confident claim.

cards.name: Relevance Ranking
cards.text: Sort competing claims by usefulness and evidence.

cards.name: Data Knows Best
cards.text: Trust the scale of the model more than human intuition.

cards.name: One More Thing
cards.text: Reveal a cleaner version of your case at the perfect time.

cards.name: Walled Garden
cards.text: Argue that restriction can protect quality and safety.

cards.name: Remove the Button
cards.text: Simplify by taking the dangerous option away.

cards.name: Alignment Pending
cards.text: Acknowledge the risk but keep developing anyway.

cards.name: Redefine AGI
cards.text: Quietly update the definition to keep your position alive.

cards.name: Probability Cloud
cards.text: Speak in calibrated uncertainty and scenario ranges.

cards.name: Maximum Compute
cards.text: Argue that scale itself unlocks the impossible.

cards.name: Digital Twin
cards.text: Model the system to predict its future behavior.

cards.name: Parallelize It
cards.text: Turn complexity into scalable workloads.

cards.name: Define Your Terms
cards.text: Clarify the key word before the argument collapses under ambiguity.

cards.name: Evidence Shield
cards.text: Protect your argument with actual support.

cards.name: Human Cost
cards.text: Force the abstract principle to touch a real person.

cards.name: Admit Uncertainty
cards.text: Earn credibility by refusing fake certainty.

cards.name: Category Error
cards.text: Separate mechanism from meaning, power from authority, or data from wisdom.

cards.name: Steelman Counter
cards.text: Represent the strongest opposing case before answering it.

cards.name: Burden of Proof
cards.text: Ask the bold claimant to do the heavy lifting.

cards.name: Moral Foundation
cards.text: Ask what grounds the moral conclusion.

cards.name: Created Value
cards.text: Defend the idea that human worth is not earned by performance.

cards.name: Worship Check
cards.text: Ask whether a tool is being asked to save, define, or rule us.

cards.name: Competing Hypothesis
cards.text: Offer another explanation that still fits the facts.

cards.name: Direct Answer
cards.text: Answer the actual question cleanly.

cards.name: Move the Goalposts
cards.text: Protect yourself by changing the standard mid-match.

cards.name: Impressive Vocabulary
cards.text: Cover weakness with prestige wording.

cards.name: Pretend That Was My Point
cards.text: Act as though the contradiction was always the plan.
